subject: biology
note: SAMPLE CBSE Class 12 / NEET-foundation questions (source 'Sample (CBSE 12)'). Replace with verified board/NEET PYQs + SME solutions + 13-language translations before shipping.

prompt.en: The structural and functional unit of the kidney is the:

solution.en: The nephron is the functional unit of the kidney; each kidney has about a million nephrons that filter blood and form urine.

prompt.en: In humans, fertilisation normally takes place in the:

solution.en: Fertilisation of the ovum by the sperm occurs in the ampulla of the fallopian tube (oviduct); the embryo then implants in the uterus.

prompt.en: Who proposed the double-helix model of DNA?

solution.en: James Watson and Francis Crick proposed the double-helix structure of DNA in 1953, using X-ray data from Rosalind Franklin.

prompt.en: Mendel's Law of Segregation states that:

choices.en: Alleles of a gene separate during gamete formation
Only dominant traits are passed on

solution.en: The Law of Segregation: the two alleles of a gene separate during gamete formation, so each gamete carries only one allele.

prompt.en: Which type of cell division produces gametes and halves the chromosome number?

solution.en: Meiosis produces four haploid gametes from one diploid cell, halving the chromosome number; mitosis keeps the number the same.

prompt.en: Which hormone lowers blood glucose levels?

solution.en: Insulin, secreted by the β-cells of the pancreas, lowers blood glucose. Glucagon does the opposite (raises it).

prompt.en: The theory of natural selection was proposed by:

solution.en: Charles Darwin proposed evolution by natural selection in 'On the Origin of Species' (1859).

prompt.en: A test cross is a cross between an organism with a dominant phenotype and one that is:

solution.en: A test cross pairs the unknown genotype with a homozygous recessive individual to reveal whether it is homozygous or heterozygous.

prompt.en: The largest gland in the human body is the:

solution.en: The liver is the largest gland (and largest internal organ) in the human body.

prompt.en: In a food chain, green plants are called:

solution.en: Green plants make their own food by photosynthesis and form the base of the food chain, so they are producers (autotrophs).
